steps.title: Identify the needed example
steps.content: The student wants to provide an example of a sky island. The notes define sky islands and quote the NPS, but the goal is narrower: the answer must name a specific mountain range identified as one of the sky islands.

steps.title: Reject general information
steps.content:
• Choice A - 'Sky islands are isolated mountain ranges whose environments differ drastically from that of the surrounding lowlands; as the NPS puts it, "the mountains are 'islands' surrounded by deserts that are 'seas.'"': defines and describes sky islands, but names no specific example.
  • Choice B - 'The NPS considers each of the sky islands, whose environments differ drastically from that of the surrounding lowlands, to be a "unique ecosystem."': reports a general NPS statement about sky islands, not an example.
  • Choice D - 'In the southwestern US and northwestern Mexico, there are dozens of sky islands: isolated mountain ranges whose environments differ drastically from that of the surrounding lowlands.': gives the region and definition, but still does not name one sky island.

steps.title: Use the named place
steps.content: Choice C - 'There are dozens of sky islands in the southwestern US and northwestern Mexico, such as the El Pinito Mountains.': uses the phrase 'such as' to present the El Pinito Mountains as a concrete example of a sky island, which directly matches the student's goal. The answer is C.
